title: Surface area of a rectangular prism
problem: A rectangular prism has length $7 \text{ cm}$, width $4 \text{ cm}$ and height $3 \text{ cm}$. Find its surface area.

steps.instruction: Write the surface area formula for a rectangular prism.
Add the terms inside the brackets.
Multiply to find the surface area.

title: Surface area of a triangular prism
problem: A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with legs $5 \text{ cm}$ and $12 \text{ cm}$. The length of the prism is $6 \text{ cm}$. Find the total surface area.

steps.instruction: Find the hypotenuse of the triangular cross-section using Pythagoras.
Find the combined area of the two triangular ends.
Find the total area of the three rectangular faces (one for each side of the triangle).
Add all face areas to find the total surface area.

title: Surface area of an open-top rectangular tray
problem: A rectangular storage tray has length $40 \text{ cm}$, width $25 \text{ cm}$ and height $15 \text{ cm}$, and has no lid. Find the total surface area of the tray.

steps.instruction: Identify the faces present. An open-top tray has 5 faces: one bottom and four sides (no top).
steps.working: Faces: bottom, two longer sides, two shorter sides

steps.instruction: Find the area of the bottom face.
Find the combined area of the two longer side faces.
Find the combined area of the two shorter side faces.
Add all face areas to find the total surface area.
